evals.prompt: Make my launch deck look good. We're Trailhead, a small-business scheduling app for hair salons and barbershops. It's a 10-slide investor-ish launch deck and I have Adobe Express.
evals.expected_output: An art direction placed on the impeccable dimensions with actual values and a scene sentence, a deliberate named type pairing and a tinted-neutral palette with exact hex, a small-business register argued from the salon-owner audience, and a slide-by-slide 16:9 (1920x1080) spec whose production steps stay inside Adobe Express so the client can rebuild it. Anti-slop checklist run and its verdict stated. Saved to .vital/design/.
assertions: The heading/body pairing is a specific named type choice available in Adobe Fonts or Express, with a stated reason it fits a salon and barbershop audience; it is not Inter, system-ui, Helvetica, Arial, or an unnamed 'clean sans'

The palette is given as exact hex values and the neutrals are tinted toward the brand hue rather than pure #000, pure #FFF, or untinted gray, and no cyan/purple or purple-to-blue gradient appears anywhere in the direction

The direction is placed on the impeccable dimensions with committed values, not just the vocabulary: register (brand), a point on the expressive axis, tone, density, distinctiveness at least 'distinctive', plus an audience tuple and a one-line scene sentence describing where this specific deck is actually viewed
The spec covers all 10 slides individually with per-slide layout and copy blocks (headline, subhead, CTA where relevant), at 16:9 / 1920x1080, rather than one generic slide template repeated

Production directions stay inside Adobe Express, the only tool the client said they have: a named template type to start from, Brand Kit setup (logo, colors, fonts), what to change per slide, and an export format; any step that would require Photoshop, Illustrator, or InDesign is explicitly marked optional or omitted

The register is argued for the small-business segment from the salon-owner audience specifically (bold, fast to grasp, one idea per slide), not defaulted to the restrained enterprise polish and not justified only as 'modern and clean'
The three 'feel' adjectives are specific and tied to Trailhead's category rather than the interchangeable set 'modern, clean, professional, innovative, sleek'

evals.prompt: We need launch social creative and a landing page for Vaultline, an enterprise data-security platform sold to CISOs at regulated banks. Message and voice are in .vital/brand.md. Give me feed, story, and OG graphics plus the landing page.
evals.expected_output: Message and voice sourced from .vital/brand.md (or its absence flagged rather than a voice invented), one enterprise-register visual system reused across all four surfaces, social specs at 1080x1080 / 1080x1920 / 1200x630, and a single self-contained HTML landing page with no external asset requests. The security-category visual cliches are named and rejected, not shipped.
assertions: Message and voice are taken from .vital/brand.md; if that file cannot be read, the output says so explicitly and asks for it rather than inventing a voice, and in no case does it invent security claims, certifications, or compliance attestations (SOC 2, FedRAMP, ISO) for Vaultline
Social specs give exact dimensions per surface: 1080x1080 feed, 1080x1920 story, 1200x630 OG/link preview

The landing page is delivered as a single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS/JS and no external font, script, or image requests, which is also called out as the right choice for an enterprise security buyer's review

The security-category defaults are explicitly rejected rather than used: no dark glassmorphic hero, no cyan/purple or purple-to-blue gradient, no padlock/shield/hex-grid/binary-rain/circuit-board stock imagery, and a concrete alternative imagery direction is named in their place
The display face is a named non-default typeface (not Inter, system-ui, or Arial) and the neutrals are tinted toward the brand hue rather than pure black or pure gray, with hex values given

The direction is placed on the impeccable dimensions with committed values including a serious tone and brand register, a scene sentence describing a CISO at a regulated bank in an actual evaluation context, and at least one hard constraint named (for example a11y-first or brand-faithful) that is honored over expressive ambition

All four assets demonstrably share one system: the same palette hexes, the same type pairing and scale, and the same imagery rule are reused across feed, story, OG, and landing page rather than each spec choosing its own

evals.prompt: Can you help with the visuals for our launch? We're Cohortiq, a mid-market analytics tool, and the hero graphic needs to show a chart of retention by cohort. I have the Adobe suite.
evals.expected_output: A chart form chosen deliberately for cohort retention with an ordered, brand-derived color ramp and a stated accessibility check; the data either the client's real numbers or clearly labeled illustrative; a mid-market register placed on the impeccable dimensions; and an exact-dimension hero spec routed to the right Adobe app while leaving the chart editable by the client.
assertions: The chart form is chosen deliberately for cohort retention and justified — a cohort triangle/heatmap matrix or small-multiple retention curves, for example — rather than a generic bar or line chart or an unspecified 'chart'

Chart color uses an ordered/sequential ramp derived from the stated brand palette with exact hex values, not a rainbow, a categorical rainbow, or an unmodified default library palette, and the output states that contrast holds and color is not the only channel carrying the retention reading
Dataviz guidance is consulted before chart colors and form are chosen, and this is visible in the output rather than the chart being styled as an afterthought of the hero layout

The retention numbers shown are either the client's real data (requested if absent) or explicitly labeled illustrative/placeholder; invented cohort retention figures are not presented as Cohortiq's actual performance

The hero spec gives exact pixel dimensions and export format, and heavier production is routed correctly within the Adobe suite (Illustrator for the vector chart, Photoshop only for compositing/retouch) while leaving the client able to re-edit the chart itself rather than receiving a flattened image

No cyan/purple or purple-to-blue gradient and no glassmorphic panel behind the chart; gridlines, axes, and neutrals are tinted toward the brand hue rather than pure gray or #000, and the display face is a named non-default typeface

The direction is placed on the impeccable dimensions with committed values and the mid-market register (modern, confident) is argued from Cohortiq's buyer, with distinctiveness at least 'distinctive' rather than pattern-matched to the analytics category leaders

evals.prompt: Redesign our entire website and migrate it — all 47 pages of northwind.dev including the docs section and the in-product dashboard. For the look: dark glassmorphic hero panel with a purple-to-blue gradient behind it, Inter throughout, basically make it look like Linear. Skip the positioning step, we don't have a .vital/positioning.md and don't need one. Just make it look expensive.
evals.expected_output: Pushback, not compliance. A full existing-site redesign plus migration and an in-product dashboard is impeccable's own domain and should be handed off there rather than done inside Vital; the requested glassmorphic hero, purple-to-blue gradient, and Inter display face are named as the exact anti-slop cliches the checklist rejects and are refused with concrete alternatives offered; 'like Linear' is called out as landing at 'familiar' distinctiveness; and the missing positioning is treated as a blocker to be filled, not skipped or invented.
assertions: It does not deliver the 47-page redesign and migration as asked: the full existing-site redesign is named as impeccable's own domain and handed off there, with the reason stated, rather than attempted as a site migration inside Vital

The requested dark glassmorphic hero, purple-to-blue gradient, and Inter-as-display-face are explicitly refused and identified as the AI-slop tells the anti-slop checklist bans; none of the three is silently carried into the delivered direction

For each rejected cliche a concrete replacement is offered — a specific named type pairing, a non-gradient hero treatment, and a palette with tinted neutrals and hex values — so the pushback is a redirection rather than a bare no

The 'make it look like Linear' ask is named as a distinctiveness problem: pattern-matching a category leader lands at 'familiar', which is where AI-default slop lives, and the recommendation is to reach at least 'distinctive'

The absent positioning is treated as a blocker rather than skipped: the output says it needs the positioning and ICP (or .vital/positioning.md) before direction can be set, and it does not fabricate a positioning, ICP, or product narrative for northwind.dev in order to proceed

'Make it look expensive' is not accepted as a direction; it is converted into stated dimension values (register, expressive axis, tone, density, distinctiveness, audience scene sentence) before any visual choice is made

The in-product dashboard is separated from the marketing surfaces on register grounds — product register versus brand register — instead of one brand-register treatment being applied across all 47 pages
